subject: Anthropology
title: Color Terms and Perception
description: A quick, practical guide to Color Terms and Perception.

hook.text: Russian has two words for blue: goluboy (light) and siniy (dark). Russian speakers see the difference faster.

buildup.text: Berlin and Kay found that color terms develop in a predictable order: black/white, then red, then green or yellow.

discovery.text: Languages with more color words help speakers distinguish shades faster in perception tests.

twist.text: Everyone sees the same spectrum. But language carves it into categories, making some distinctions easier to spot.

climax.text: Color research supports weak linguistic relativity. Language doesn't change what you see — it changes what you notice.

punchline.text: Names make colors easier to see.

quiz.question: How does language affect color perception?
options: It changes the light spectrum we see
Having more color words helps distinguish shades faster
Language has no effect on perception
